dir.description: Relative path to the folder containing all the plates for the test. This is used to construct the full path to each plate by combining BaseUrl and PlatePath. For example, if BaseUrl is "Letter_Single", and PlatePath is "ishihara_A.svg", the full path to the plate will be "Letter_Single/ishihara_A.svg". This allows for better organization of plate files and easier management of file paths. For example, "General1/Letter", or "c:/Data/", or "https://example.com/plates/Test1".

ColorBlindTest.description: Represents a color blindness test consisting of a title and a collection of test plates.

dir.description: Generally empty, however, it could be relative path to the folder containing all the plates for the test. For example, "../../somewhere".

plates.description: all plates in the test. The order of plates is important, as it will be used to determine the sequence of presentation to the user.

maxPlates.description: Equal or less than the number of plates in the test. If set, the app will only present the first MaxPlates plates to the user, and ignore the rest. This is useful for quick screening tests. When set nonn zero, the plates will be randomized, and only the first MaxPlates plates will be used for the test. When set to zero, all plates will be used for the test in order, unless RandomizePlates is true.

Plate.description: Describe how to present a plate to the user, and how the user can answer. The answer can be through clicking buttons, or key-in answer through virtual keyboard, or voice input, or drawing on the screen.

platePath.description: Relative file path to the plate. e.g. Letter_Single/ishihara_A.svg

answer.description:
User input answer through selecting a button on the screen, or key-in the answer through a virtual keyboard. e.g. A, LTE
If InputMethod is ImageButtons, the answer could be General1/Letters/A.svg

buttonTexts.description: If InputMethod is ImageButtons, each button text is a (relative) file path to the source image.

description.description: Optional description and instruction for the plate. This can be used to provide additional information to the user about how to answer the plate, or any other relevant information. For example, "Please select the letter you see in the image", or "Please enter the number you see in the image". This can help improve the user experience and ensure that users understand how to interact with the plate correctly. If not defined, the description of the container TestContent will be used. If that is also not defined, the app can use a default description based on the PlateNature and InputMethod. For example, for a plate with PlateNature of Letter and InputMethod of Buttons, the default description could be "Please select the letter you see in the image". For a plate with PlateNature of Digit_2 and InputMethod of NumPad, the default description could be "Please enter the two-digit number you see in the image". The app can have a set of predefined descriptions for different combinations of PlateNature and InputMethod to ensure that users receive clear instructions on how to answer each plate.

PlateNature.description: This will determine how the UI present the way of answering

InputMethod.description: Specifies the available input methods for user interaction within the application.
